reviews.review_description: This game used to be fun when the humans were different and don’t get me wrong. It’s still fun. The only thing that I hate is kind of the things that made the game fun but the size I hate the size. The size is one of the things that made it fun. I was big and tall and humans. They look weird. It doesn’t make me feel like a monster makes me feel like a plushy toy plus this game will be better if it doesn’t depend on being goofy…￼￼

The simple, fun graphics are actually perfect for this game. They keep the tone light while you're busy leveling skyscrapers. What truly shines is the incredible interactivity—every building, every car, every lamppost is just waiting for you to unleash a monster move on it. The different weapons add a layer of strategy to your destruction. A well-designed chaos simulator!

way too laggy i love the concept just please my game keeps crashing i have amazing wifi and i have plenty of storage please fix the lag and also keep not letting me even complete level one no matter how many enemies i kill and how much stuff i do i kill the big ship i break every single thing i do everything in it doesn't do anything i'm just stuck

developer_response.body:
Hi there,
We’re truly sorry for the frustrating experience you’ve had with the lag, crashes, and being stuck early on. That’s absolutely not the fun we want you to have, and we take your feedback very seriously.

We’ve already released an update that fixes the lag and crashing issues, and we’ve also improved controls, movement, and overall gameplay smoothness to make it feel much better.

Our game is designed as an open sandbox experience, so there’s no fixed “level 1” you need to complete. Instead, you’re free to explore, discover, and unlock new items and achievements at your own pace. There are over 100 achievements, more than 10 items, and 3 upgradable skills waiting for you to explore. We’ll also be adding new monster characters and more unlockable content in future updates to expand the game even further.

We understand the game is still in its early stages, and we’re working hard every day to polish and improve it. Thank you so much for your patience and support — it means a lot to us. We’ll keep listening, updating, and making this game better in the new year, and we hope you’ll give it another try soon.

Thank you for caring enough to share your thoughts with us.

reviews.review_description: It is a pile of poo

developer_response.body:
hello Marley,

We are sad to hear you dislike the game. Can you tell me which part makes you unhappy about the game?

We put our heart and soul into the game, and want to improve it if you can give us more feedback, thanks!!

reviews.review_title: A solid monster simulator with great phy
reviews.review_description:
​The physics engine in this game is surprisingly solid—the haptic feedback when you grab objects or smash through structures feels very impactful and immersive.
​What makes it stand out is the rich variety of environmental interactions. It's not just about toppling buildings; there are so many interactive props and elements across the map that you can use to your advantage. This "sandbox" feel gives the game a lot of replay value, as you're constantly finding new ways to wreck the city. Watching the skyline crumble under your feet is incredibly satisfying. A top-tier choice for fans of the giant monster genre!

Now that you can become giant it truly feels like a kaiju simulator and the other powers are great too. However it would be a 5 star if you could destroy everything since there are some buildings that cannot be destroyed.

This game totally nails my monster fantasy—transforming into Godzilla or King Kong to wreck the city is insane!
The VR immersion is killer—crushing cars and smashing buildings feels crazy real! My favorite part? Climbing skyscrapers and roaring like Kong—ignites my inner chuunibyo!
Wish the movement speed was faster for extra chaos，Overall, it’s insanely cool!

developer_response.body:
Hey there! 🎉
THANK YOU for this awesome hype—we’re so stoked to hear the monster fantasy is hitting exactly the vibe we wanted! There’s nothing better than knowing climbing skyscrapers, roaring like Kong, and turning cities into your playground feels that immersive in VR—chuunibyo mode activated, and we’re HERE FOR IT. 😂
Great call on the movement speed tweak! We’ve actually had a few players ask for a “chaos boost” option, and it’s 100% on our radar for the next update. Imagine tearing through streets faster to level more blocks? Yeah, we’re already hyped to make that happen.
Keep the feedback coming—you’re the reason we keep refining this monster mayhem. Go unleash that inner kaiju some more, and stay tuned for updates! 🦖🦍

reviews.review_description:
Monster Me is a jubilant VR playground for those who love uninhibited destruction. 
Accessible, funny, and full of interactive surprises, it's a sandbox game that delivers a truly cathartic release in the world of VR!

reviews.review_title: This VR game has me SCREAMING
reviews.review_description: literally & figuratively 😂 giant monster vibes? Stomping cars like they’re soda cans? Punching skyscrapers into confetti? SIGN ME UP. As Intern Monster #133458, I’m over here yeeting humans with my paws, crashing airships like a chaos gremlin, and farming “scream energy” like it’s TikTok clout 🚀 The quests are wild—swapping landmarks? Pranking citizens till they cry-laugh? This open-world Crunch Town’s my playground! Oh and the gadgets? Mjölnir that boomerangs back?? Freeze blaster to ice skate on streets?? I’m over here cackling like a maniac while my AHHH Coins stack up. If you wanna live out your Godzilla-meets-Jackass fantasies, this is *the* vibe. 10/10 would destroy again 🔥 #MonsterGoals #ChaosIsMyCalling

developer_response.body:
Intern Monster #133458—YOU ARE THE CHAOS WE NEED 😂🔥
Yeeting humans, crashing airships, farming scream energy like TikTok clout, swapping landmarks and pranking citizens till they cry-laugh? This is exactly the unhinged, cackle-till-your-throat-hurts fun we built Crunch Town for.
And don’t even get us started on Mjölnir boomerangs and ice-skating streets with the freeze blaster—we’re screaming right there with you.
10/10, keep being the chaos gremlin legend you are. The AHHH Coins (and the destruction) will keep coming!
The Dev Team

reviews.review_description: DO NOT BUY THIS GAME, I though it would be fun but there is this terrible music that can’t be disabled. You feel puny the physics are mid overall it sucks.

developer_response.body:
Hi there,
We’re truly sorry this game didn’t live up to your expectations—your feedback means a lot, and we’re taking every word seriously.

The unskippable music is a critical oversight, and we’ve updated our setting menu where you can turn on/off the music. Also we have updated both the home base BGM and the city BGM in our XMAS update - hope this one feels better for you! 

We’re also revisiting the physics and character feel to tighten up the responsiveness that’s missing right now. We hope you understand this will take some time but will definitely improve over time.

Thanks for calling these issues out—they’ll help us make the game better for everyone.

The Dev Team

reviews.review_title: This game perfectly fulfills my dream
reviews.review_description: This game perfectly fulfills my dream of being Godzilla! Putting on the VR headset, I truly feel like I've become a 40-meter-tall giant. The haptic feedback from stomping on cars and punching through buildings feels incredibly real. The overwhelming sense of overlooking the entire city and doing whatever you want is something you can't get from regular screen games.

developer_response.body:
This review made our whole team’s day—thank you so much for sharing!
We set out to make that 40-meter giant power trip feel real, and it means the world that the VR immersion and haptic feedback hit that sweet spot for you. Stomping cars, punching skyscrapers, and owning the city—this is exactly the Godzilla-level chaos we dreamed of when building this game.
So stoked we could turn your dream into a VR reality. Keep crushing those skyscrapers!
The Dev Team

reviews.review_description: The freedom and destructive fun are off the charts! The game doesn't have too many restrictions; you just get into the map and go wild. While I wish the movement speed could be faster, the moment I climbed to the top of a skyscraper and roared like King Kong, all my inner chuunibyo was ignited!

developer_response.body:
Hell yeah—this is the chaotic, no-rules fun we live for! 🦍
So stoked the freedom and destructive vibes hit for you, and that skyscraper roar moment? Total chuunibyo perfection, we’re obsessed.
We hear you loud and clear on the movement speed tweak—we’ll add that to our next update’s priority list. Keep going wild out there!
The Dev Team

reviews.review_description: Using various props to destroy towns, most items and buildings can be broken, acting recklessly and without restraint. Hope to update more props and different monsters
Play as a monster, smash cities. Simple, fun graphics. Lots of weapons. Very relaxing and easy to get into. Highly recommend for pure mayhem!

Originally just wanted to give it a try, but it's unexpectedly fun. I really like the loop of following missions, earning money, and collecting different items. It gives a great sense of achievement. I personally really enjoy this kind of objective-based destruction.

developer_response.body:
Hey friend!
We’re thrilled to hear the collection loop hooked you in—there’s nothing better than that satisfying grind of knocking out missions, cashing in rewards, and adding new gear to your stash. That “objective-based destruction” vibe was exactly what we were going for—chaos with a purpose, right? 😎
So stoked the sense of achievement landed for you—keep leveling up that collection, and stay tuned for more destructible fun down the line!

reviews.review_description: The destruction feels absolutely amazing! Watching buildings crumble and tossing cars around like toys feels so satisfying. The physics are great. This is exactly the kind of stress relief I needed. Highly recommended for anyone looking to blow off some steam!

developer_response.body:
Hey there!
It makes our day to hear this game is your ultimate stress reliever—that’s exactly why we built it! There’s nothing more satisfying than turning a bad day around by smashing buildings and hurling cars like they’re toys, and we’re stoked the physics landed that chaotic, satisfying feel you love.
Shoutout to all the players who use this game to blow off steam—keep wrecking, and thanks for the glowing recommendation! 🦖

reviews.review_description: Very happy! I really feel like I'm becoming a 'Godzilla' and doing whatever I want! If you follow the task, you can experience and unlock more gameplay with the props you get. Overall, I think it's super cool! Haha, I'm a big monster!

developer_response.body:
Hey there! So stoked to see you having this much fun—“becoming Godzilla and doing whatever I want” is exactly the vibe we dreamed of when making this game!
We’re grinning ear to ear reading your words—nothing hits harder than seeing players fully lean into the chaos and joy of being a giant monster. And hey, keep grabbing those props right now—because a huge version update is coming soon! We’ve got a brand-new prop shop, tons more missions, and a bunch of fresh skill gameplay waiting for you to tear into.

Thanks for diving in and bringing this monster fantasy to life with us. Can’t wait to see what new chaos you unleash when the update drops—go crush it, big guy! 🦖

reviews.review_description: Mid game not as fun as it looks 😒

developer_response.body:
Hi there,
We’re truly sorry to hear you don’t enjoy the game and that it didn’t live up to your expectations. We totally understand how disappointing it is when a game doesn’t feel as fun as it looks, and we take your feedback very seriously.

We’re already hard at work on improving core gameplay, making it more engaging, dynamic, and satisfying. We’ll be refining combat, controls, progression, and content variety to make every moment feel more meaningful and fun. We also plan to add more unique challenges, rewards, events, and content updates to keep the game fresh and exciting over time.

The game is still being actively improved, and we’re committed to making it deeper, more polished, and truly worth your time. Thank you so much for giving it a try — we really hope you’ll give us another chance in future updates as we keep making the game better and better.

Yours,
Team Monster

reviews.review_title: Transform into a monster and wreak havoc
reviews.review_description: It's a really fun game; you can destroy everything you see. It would be even more fun if the movement speed were faster. When I climbed to the top of the building, I felt like I had become King Kong.

developer_response.body:
Hey there!
We’re hyped to hear you loved transforming into a monster and tearing through everything in sight—that’s the chaos we live for! And climbing to the top of the building and feeling like King Kong? That’s exactly the immersive vibe we wanted to nail for our players.
Great call on the movement speed tweak! We’ve had a bunch of players ask for a faster, more chaotic pace, and it’s actually on our list of updates to refine. Imagine zipping through the city even quicker to level more buildings—we’re already stoked to make that happen.
Keep the feedback coming, and keep wreaking that awesome havoc! 🦍

reviews.review_description: Absolute brain-dead fun in the best way possible.

reviews.review_title: If you break them, you'll be praised
reviews.review_description: Just break everything you see. If you break down certain buildings and get them in the news, you'll get coins and buy items to break them better with. Everything is perfectly physically implemented, so you can even crawl around with both hands. I recommend this to players who want a simple, uncomplicated action.

jump out of my balloon and just want to do the achievements to get my coins so I can buy upgrades.
Climb buildings, destroy some, play some basketball,.. did already some….

A sandbox game with a lot of challenges to fullfill.

The game feels much slower than the videos show.  Go around smash some buildings, hear some people scream, wait for the lag to stop, repeat.  
I found the writing quite small to read and a lot of the graphics when played on a quest 3 look very poorly rendered.  
Might play it one or two more times but I didn't feel like there was a 'oh, what's going to happen next?'

reviews.review_title: I am the King of Godzilla!
reviews.review_description: A destructive and stress-relieving VR game with simple and easy-to-learn controls, excellent optimization, rich city maps, and smooth, lag-free gameplay. I am the King of Godzilla!

developer_response.body:
Hey there, King of Godzilla 👑
We’re so stoked to hear you’re owning that kaiju throne—and that the smooth, lag-free gameplay and rich city maps hit just right for your destructive, stress-relieving sessions. Simple controls and solid optimization were huge priorities for us, so it means the world to know they’re working for you.
Keep roaring, keep smashing, and long live the King! 🦖

reviews.review_description: When I first got the game I was excited to play. But yesterday I could not leave the airship

developer_response.body:
Hello Sharkattack66340!

We have updated a new version to fix this problem. Can you do an update and see if the issue is solved? Sorry for the previous bug!

Best,
Monster ME Developer team

reviews.review_title: My boss told me to destroy the city.
reviews.review_description: I excelled  Finally, a game that understands my inner chaos. As an intern monster, my job is to smash everything, and Monster:Medelivers. The POV destruction is incredibly cathartic, and I love trying out all the different weapons on buildings. The simple, fun graphics make the rampage feel guilt-free. Forget anger management, just play this. 10/10 would destroy again.

If you want to feel like an unstoppable force, this game is a 10/10. The environmental interaction is insane—the city is your toy box, and everything is breakable! I love how many interactive props are scattered around the map. Grab a car, smash a helicopter out of the sky, or just level an entire skyscraper with your bare hands.
The physics mechanics are super satisfying and add a real punch to every smash and grab. It’s so much fun finding new ways to wreck the city using the different tools and objects you find. For a monster sim, the variety of interactions is top-tier. Highly recommended for anyone who needs to vent some steam and cause some serious chaos! 🚀🏢

developer_response.body:
Thank you so much for this amazing review—we’re absolutely thrilled to hear you’re loving the chaotic, destructive fun of our game! 🎉
Reading that you rate it 10/10 and enjoy the environmental interaction, destructible city, physics, and all the interactive props means the world to our team. We designed it exactly for players like you who want to feel like an unstoppable force and let loose some chaos!
We’re already hard at work making the game even better: we’ll keep polishing performance, fixing any lag or stability issues, expanding gameplay depth, adding new monsters, more destructible elements, and even more satisfying ways to cause chaos.
Thank you again for your support—it truly motivates us to keep building the ultimate destruction playground! We can’t wait to show you what’s coming next.

reviews.review_title: My go-to game for a quick mental break.
reviews.review_description: Sometimes you just need to turn off your brain and smash stuff. Monster:Me is exactly that. The easygoing atmosphere and simple, fun graphics make it incredibly relaxing. Just stomping around, trying out various weapons, and watching the city crumble from a giant smonster's POV is surprisingly therapeutic. Zero stress, 100% fun.
Good fun and interactive, however it would have been improved If the monster you play was to get bigger the more you developed.

developer_response.body:
Thanks for playing and for your kind words—really glad you had fun and found it interactive!

Great news: in the latest update, we’ve added a skill system that lets your monster grow bigger as you level up. The bigger you get, the more powerful abilities and new gameplay you unlock. Hope you enjoy the new version!

reviews.review_description: Playing as a monster, wreaking havoc freely in the city, purchasing novel gadgets, complemented by a quite impressive physics interaction system—grabbing a massive truck and hurling it toward a skyscraper made me feel like I was King Kong.

The quests are wild too—yesterday I swapped the town clock tower with a giant rubber duck and got SO many AHHHH Coins 🦆💰 Now I’m over here zapping streets with a freeze blaster and launching folks with a slingshot like it’s a monster carnival 🎢❄️ The physics? Insane. Everything goes BOOM or SPLAT or slides around like a slipp

developer_response.body:
Wow, those quest stories are chef’s kiss—swapping the clock tower for a giant rubber duck? Total chaos in the best way, and those AHHHH Coins are well earned! 🦆💰 Freeze blasters, slingshot launches, monster carnival vibes—this is exactly the wild, messy fun we dreamed of when designing these quests.
So glad the physics are hitting that insane sweet spot of BOOM, SPLAT, and slippery chaos for you. Thanks for bringing this game to life with your epic plays—we’re grinning over here!
The Dev Team

reviews.review_title: This is very interesting and very relaxi
reviews.review_description: It feels very relaxing and is a very fun and interesting VR game. Playing it after work can sweep away the troubles of work and life. The gameplay is also very interesting and creative, and overall, it feels very enjoyable.

Physics? *Chef’s kiss x2*. Citizens bounce like beach balls, buildings collapse like Jenga towers, and my airship crash-landing? Pure cinematic gold 🎥. This ain’t just a game—it’s a viral TikTok factory.

developer_response.body:
Hey there!
Huge thanks for the awesome review—this made our whole team’s day!
We’re stoked you loved the physics so much! Tuning those bouncy citizen ragdolls, Jenga-style building collapses, and cinematic airship crashes was so worth it to see you hype about it.
And “viral TikTok factory”? That’s the ultimate compliment—we live for those shareable, chaotic VR moments.
Thanks for playing, and can’t wait for you to check out what’s next!
The Dev Team
